transcript: Here's what you need to know before taking oil of oregano. Oil of oregano can actually harm you if you don't know this. And if you don't believe me, I'm a pancreatic cancer researcher, so biology is my thing. Oregano oil is nature's strongest antimicrobial agent. It removes parasites, bacteria, fungi, viruses, everything. That most people are taking it wrong. And there are three things you need to know before you take another capsule. Number one, the active ingredient in oregano oil is called carvacrol. And here's the scary part. If you're taking a brand with weak carvacrol, anything under 85%, you're not killing the parasites. You're feeding them just enough to make them angry. They don't die, they adapt, they multiply faster, and now they're harder to kill than before you started. Number two, you should always take oregano oil with black seed oil. Here's why. When parasites die, they release toxins into your body. That's called die off. It's why so many people try oregano oil and feel terrible. Headaches, brain fog, nausea, exhaustion. They think the oregano isn't agreeing with them, but it's actually the dead parasites releasing poison with nowhere to go. Black seed oil flushes them out so you don't get that awful reaction. Number three, oregano oil goes bad really fast once it's exposed to air. It's like when you cut an apple and it turns brown. Same thing happens to oregano oil. It breaks down and stops working. Most brands use soft gels or droppers that let air in every time you open them. So even if the label says a high carvacrol strength, by week two, it's basically useless. You need something that's sealed from oxygen completely.
